Free printable · from The Cardinal's Toolkit

The right words when a child is grieving.

Children grieve in bursts, through play, with questions that catch you off guard. This free one-pager, Helping a Child Through Grief, gives you simple, honest language and small ways to help — no perfect words required.

  • 🧸 Clear, gentle words to use (and ones to avoid)
  • 🧸 What grief looks like at different ages
  • 🧸 Answers to the questions children ask
  • 🧸 When to seek extra help

Get it free

Enter your email and we'll send the guide — plus the occasional gentle note. Unsubscribe any time.

We'll only email you about the books and family grief resources. Your address is never sold or shared.

“You don't have to take their pain away to help. Staying close, telling the truth gently, and letting them feel — that is the whole job.” — The Cardinal's Toolkit

Prefer to read it first? You can open the guide here — or see all free downloads.
